title: 2 Terminology
body_text:
2	Terminology
2.1	Definitions
Branch Number
The Branch Number is a 5-digit number used to identify the branch of a Canadian financial institution. The Branch Number and the Institution Number make up the Transit Number.
Centralized Cheque Returns
Centralized Cheque Returns is the process of using a central archive or storage facility so that credit unions can select which cheques to dishonour online. Central 1 initiates the cheque return with archived Images or physical items.
Chargeback
A Chargeback is an item that the credit union (the negotiating financial institution) deposits that is subsequently dishonoured by the Drawee financial institution.
Cheque Image Statements
Cheque Image Statements are account statements which have Images of cheques printed as part of the statement and provided to the member, instead of the physical cheques being enclosed. Cheque Image Statements are provided by the credit union or the credit unionâs third party statement processor.
Clearing
Clearing is the system exchanging and reconciling payment items that result in a transfer of funds from one financial institution to another.
Client Centre
The Client Centre is a digital portal that provides secured access to Central 1âs applications and to information and news about our products and services.
Client Centre Users
Client Centre Users log in to the Client Centre to access certain applications. Client Centre Users canât manage CPS Admin Security Officers or other Client Centre Users.
CPA
CPA means Canadian Payments Association, also known as Payments Canada.
CPS Admin
The Central Portal System (CPS) Admin application is used to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users and CPS Admin Security Officers.
CPS Admin Security Officer
CPS Admin Security Officers use the CPS Admin or the User Management application to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users. They can also delete and modify other CPS Admin Security Officers (with some exceptions) via the User Management application. CPS Admin Security Officers use the Client Centre to log in to certain applications (e.g., Cash Services).
Drawee
A Drawee is the financial institution to which a debit payment item is addressed. The Drawee is directed to pay the specified amount of money.
Drawer
A Drawer is the person who signs the cheque ordering the Drawee financial institution to pay the specified amount of money stated on it.
DREC File (BC Only)
The DREC File is one of the file formats used for Canadian dollar Qualified Items drawn on the credit union or on members of the credit union. This file facilitates automatic settlement to the account on which the items are drawn.
File and Report Exchange
The File and Report Exchange allows authorized users to download files from Central 1 and upload files to Central 1. Users can connect to the File and Report Exchange via direct connection (SFTP) or online via the Client Centre.
Image
An Image is a digital representation of the front and back of a payment item.
Image File
An Image File is a file that Central 1 provides to credit unions or their service providers of cleared cheques drawn on membersâ accounts. These cheques can be included in membersâ statements.
Image Printout
An Image Printout is any paper output of an Image created by a financial institution using CPA rules for the creation and storage of Images.
Incoming Clearing Item
An Incoming Clearing Item is a cheque, draft, or money order that is drawn on the credit union or a member of the credit union, deposited by the Payee, and sent through the Clearing for debit to the Drawerâs account.
Institution Number
The Institution Number is a 3-digit number assigned by the CPA that identifies a financial institution (e.g., 809 for BC credit unions and 0828 for most Ontario credit unions). The Branch Number and the Institution Number make up the Transit Number. The Institution Number is sometimes known as the route number.
MICR
Magnetic Ink Character Recognition (MICR) means characters printed with ink that contains particles of a magnetic material that allows for machine recognition.
Posting File (Ontario Only)
The Posting File is one of the file formats used for Qualified Items drawn on the credit union or members of the credit union. The file facilitates automatic settlement to the account on which the items are drawn. There is a Canadian dollar Posting File and a US dollar Posting File.
Qualified Item
Qualified Items are cheques or drafts that meet CPA standards and are MICR encoded with the Transit Number and dollar value. Refer to CPA, Standard 006, Specifications for MICR-Encoded Payment Items.
QREC File (BC Only)
The QREC File is a file of US dollar Qualified Items drawn on the credit union or on members of the credit union. This file facilitates automatic settlement to the accounts on which the items are drawn.
Return Replacement Document
A Return Replacement Document (RRD) is an official document that meets the specification in CPA Standard 013, Return Replacement Document Design Standard. It can be used for the purpose of return in accordance with CPA Rule A10, Images, Image Printouts and Return Replacement Documents.
Transit Number
The Transit Number is made up of the Institution Number together with the Branch Number (e.g., 80912010).
Unqualified Item
Unqualified Items are cheques or drafts that do not meet CPA standards for a Qualified Item, or cannot be imaged by Central 1âs imaging equipment (e.g., the item is too damaged). Central 1 determines whether an item is qualified or unqualified.
User Management
User Management is an application used to add, edit, and delete Client Centre Users. It is also used to edit and delete other CPS Admin Security Officers and to manage 2-step security tokens. Eventually, User Management will replace CPS Admin.
END
